sources_audited.note: The cited BLS URL returned an automated-access denial, so its NAICS definition and occupational context could not be independently inspected in this review. This does not create a material finding because the affected researched inputs are already proxy or estimate judgments and an honest downgrade would not change the MODEL_CONDITIONED action.

The cited BLS projections page returned an automated-access denial, preventing independent verification of the quoted 2024-2034 occupational counts and declines. The resulting source weakness is nonmaterial under the stated test because a and d5 could be retained only as bounded estimates without changing the record's MODEL_CONDITIONED action or its base-tier mechanism.

The April 2024 executive summary is accessible and supports industry concern with labor costs, cost and quality control, productivity, automation, business intelligence, e-commerce portals, production automation, and growing AI use. Its 191-company printing-industry sample is broader than independent NAICS 323120 firms and does not directly measure the packet's task exposure, implementation, retention, demand, or operator-required shares.

Gallup reports that 22% of employer-business owners planned to sell or transfer ownership within five years, while 5% planned closure and 5% had no plan. The packet transparently treats this all-industry intention measure as a proxy rather than completed NAICS 323120 transfers.

caveats: S1 could not be independently inspected because the BLS page denied automated access; its claimed taxonomy and occupational context therefore remain unverified in this review.
S2 could not be independently inspected because the BLS page denied automated access; the quoted occupational projection counts and decline rates therefore remain unverified in this review.
S3 surveys a broad printing-industry panel rather than independent NAICS 323120 support providers and supports automation direction, not the numerical levels assigned to a, rho, q, d5, or o.
S4 measures broad employer-owner intentions rather than completed, qualifying control transfers in this industry; the packet discloses this limitation and discounts the proxy.
summary: Mechanics pass. The record is useful and internally coherent, uses the default lens, keeps implementation, retention, demand, and operator-required constructs separated, and honestly labels its score-driving judgments as PROXY or ESTIMATE with wide ranges and explicit change evidence. The accessible industry and succession sources support the stated directional premises, but the evidence is broad rather than six-digit-specific, and both BLS pages were blocked from independent inspection. Those weaknesses warrant caveats but do not meet the frozen materiality test: correcting or honestly downgrading them would not change the CONDITIONAL base-tier mechanism or the already MODEL_CONDITIONED/VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS action.
